items.title: China’s AI Boom Could Outlast the U.S. Why Alibaba’s Bet Is Just the Start.
items.description: Alibaba  Group Holding is reminding investors that the U.S. isn’t the only AI game in town.  It’s the latest reminder that China’s AI boom could play out differently—and last longer—than in the U.S.  Alibaba  CEO Eddie Wu said the company would expand its investment on AI and cloud infrastructure over three years beyond the 380 billion yuan it outlined in February, as demand for AI infrastructure has far outstripped the company’s expectations.

items.title: Waymo Unveils Corporate Self-Driving Taxis. The Technology Is Spreading.
items.description: Self-driving technology company Waymo announced a new autonomous driving service on Wednesday targeted at businesses.  “Waymo for Business” touts efficiency, allowing businesses signed up for the taxi platform to integrate it into their employee workflows such as travel apps and expense reporting.  The idea is for businesses to use the tool to offer rides to employees, building a recurring business travel operation that also gives employers control over where and how the offering is used.
